revision.text:
[[commons:Category:Ichinomiya Sengen Shrine]][[en:Ichinomiya Sengen Shrine]][[ja:一宮浅間神社]] {{Expand Japanese|一宮浅間神社}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Ichinomiya Sengen Shrine | native_name = 一宮浅間神社 | image = Ichinomiya-sengen Shrine in Ichikawamisato, Yamanashi, Japan - 2010.jpg | mapframe = yes | mapframe-zoom = 15 | mapframe-wikidata = yes | mapframe-point = none | map_type = Japan | map_caption = Ichinomiya Sengen Shrine | coordinates = {{coord|35.5542|138.49016111111112}} | map_relief = 1 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | deity = {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|lt=Konohananosakuya-bime|az|Konohanasakuya|ca|Konohana no Sakuya hime|de|Konohanasakuyahime|en|Konohanasakuya-hime|fa|کونوهاناساکویا-هیمه|fr|Konohana-no-Sakuya|ja|コノハナノサクヤビメ|ko|고노하나노사쿠야비메|mni|ꯀꯣꯅꯣꯍꯅꯁꯀꯨꯌ-ꯍꯤꯃꯦ|pl|Konohanasakuya-hime|ru|Коноханасакуя-химэ|sv|Konohanasakuyahime|th|เซ็นเง็ง|vi|Konohana Sakuya Hime|zh|木花開耶姬|qid=Q1781862|1=Konohanasakuya-hime}} }} {{nihongo|'''Ichinomiya Sengen Shrine'''|一宮浅間神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Kai Province]]. == instance of (P31) == * {{ill|Sengen shrine|lt=Sengen shrine|en|Sengen shrine|fa|زیارتگاه سنگن|fr|Sanctuaires Asama|it|Santuario di Asama|ja|浅間神社|sv|Asama-helgedom|zh|淺間神社|qid=Q3471785|1=Sengen shrine}} * {{ill|Regional Ichinomiya|lt=Regional Ichinomiya|qid=Q134917290|1=Regional Ichinomiya}} * {{ill|Myojin Taisha|lt=Myōjin Taisha|en|Myojin Taisha|fr|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|simple|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|qid=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}} * {{ill|Q134917286|ltq=Q134917286|qid=Q134917286|1=Shikinaisha}} * {{ill|Q845945|ltq=Q845945|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} * {{ill|Ichinomiya|lt=ichinomiya|ca|Ichinomiya (Xinto)|de|Ichi-no-miya|en|Ichinomiya|fr|Ichi-no-miya|id|Ichinomiya|ja|一宮|ko|이치노미야|pt|Ichinomiya (templo)|simple|Ichinomiya|th|อิจิโนมิยะ|uk|Ітіномія (титул)|zh|一宮|qid=Q1656379|1=Ichinomiya}} * {{ill|Q135022918|ltq=Q135022918|qid=Q135022918|1=Shikinai Successor}} * {{ill|Q135026601|ltq=Q135026601|qid=Q135026601|1=Shikinai Hiteisha}} == part of (P361) == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Kai Province|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Kai Province|ja|甲斐国の式内社一覧|qid=Q11577868|1=List of Shikinaisha in Kai Province}} == located in the administrative territorial entity (P131) == * {{ill|Q1204453|ltq=Q1204453|qid=Q1204453|1=Ichikawamisato}} == coordinate location (P625) == * {{coord|35.5542|138.49016111111112}} == image (P18) == * Ichinomiya-sengen Shrine in Ichikawamisato, Yamanashi, Japan - 2010.jpg == dedicated to (P825) == * {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|lt=Konohananosakuya-bime|az|Konohanasakuya|ca|Konohana no Sakuya hime|de|Konohanasakuyahime|en|Konohanasakuya-hime|fa|کونوهاناساکویا-هیمه|fr|Konohana-no-Sakuya|ja|コノハナノサクヤビメ|ko|고노하나노사쿠야비메|mni|ꯀꯣꯅꯣꯍꯅꯁꯀꯨꯌ-ꯍꯤꯃꯦ|pl|Konohanasakuya-hime|ru|Коноханасакуя-химэ|sv|Konohanasakuyahime|th|เซ็นเง็ง|vi|Konohana Sakuya Hime|zh|木花開耶姬|qid=Q1781862|1=Konohanasakuya-hime}} == described by source (P1343) == * {{ill|Q135037878|ltq=Q135037878|qid=Q135037878|1=Kokugakuin University Shrine database (old)}} ** P2699: https://web.archive.org/web/20060723061528/http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp:80/db/jinja/146001.html == modern shrine ranking (P13723) == * {{ill|Son-sha|lt=Son-sha|ja|村社|qid=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} ** End time: {'time': '+1946-02-02T00:00:00Z', 'timezone': 0, 'before': 0, 'after': 0, 'precision': 11, 'calendarmodel': 'http://www.wikidata.org/entity/Q1985727'} ** P1534: {{ill|Q3029647|ltq=Q3029647|qid=Q3029647|1=Shinto Directive}} == country (P17) == * {{ill|Q17|ltq=Q17|qid=Q17|1=Japan}} == native label (P1705) == * {'text': '一宮浅間神社', 'language': 'ja'} ** P2125: Ichinomiya Sengen Jinja == name in kana (P1814) == * いちのみやせんげんじんじゃ == different from (P1889) == * {{ill|Ichinomiya Asama Shrine (Fuefuki)|lt=Ichinomiya Asama Shrine|en|Ichinomiya Asama Shrine (Fuefuki)|ja|浅間神社 (笛吹市)|zh|淺間神社 (笛吹市)|qid=Q11557476|1=Ichinomiya Asama Shrine (Fuefuki)}} == postal code (P281) == * 409-3606 == TripAdvisor ID (P3134) == * 1368802 == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 4090005005156 == Commons category (P373) == * Ichinomiya Sengen Shrine == said to be the same as (P460) == * {{ill|Q135039708|ltq=Q135039708|qid=Q135039708|1=Asamano Shrine}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '山梨県西八代郡市川三郷町高田字宮本3696', 'language': 'ja'} * {'text': '3696 Miyamoto, Takata, Ichikawamisato-chō, Nishiyatsushiro-gun, Yamanashi-ken 409-3606', 'language': 'en'} * {'text': '山梨県笛吹市一宮町一ノ宮', 'language': 'ja'} == image of entrance (P9721) == * Ichinomiya-sengen Shrine C-2010.jpg {{commons category|Category:Ichinomiya Sengen Shrine}} [[EN:Ichinomiya Sengen Shrine]] [[JA:一宮浅間神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11352589}} == Japanese content == {{otheruses|Ichinomiya Sengen Shrine, Ichikawamisato Town, Yamanashi Prefecture|Sengen Shrine in Ichinomiya, Kai Province|Sengen Shrine (Fuefuki City)}} {{Shrine |Name = Ichinomiya Sengen Shrine |Image = [[File:Ichinomiya-sengen Shrine A-2010.jpg|280px]]
Shrine
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=280}} |Location = {{ill|lt=Yamanashi Prefecture|Q132720|qid=Q132720|1=Yamanashi Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Nishiyashiro District|Q1131467|qid=Q1131467|1=Nishiyatsushiro District, Yamanashi}}{{ill|lt=Ichikawa Misato Town|Q1204453|qid=Q1204453|1=Ichikawamisato}}3696 Miyamoto, Takada |Position = {{coord|35|33|15.12|N|138|29|24.58|E|region:JP-19_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deity = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Konohana Sakuyahime no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} |Divinity = |Shakaku = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}} ({{ill|lt=Meishin Taisha|Q9610964|qid=Q9610964|lt=Meishindai|1=Myōjin Taisha}}) {{ill|lt=Ronsha|Q135022904|qid=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}}
Old {{ill|lt=Murasha|Q134917284|qid=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |Foundation = (tradition) {{ill|lt=Emperor Keiko|Q329723|qid=Q329723|1=Emperor Keikō}} years |Main shrine = Sangensha {{ill|lt=Rifted construction|Q210684|qid=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}}Copper plate roof |alias=|Fudasho, etc. = |Regular festival = {{ill|lt=November 3rd|Q2995|qid=Q2995|1=November 3}} |Shinto ritual = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Yamanashi|label=Ichinomiya
Sengen Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} }} '''Ichinomiya Sengen Shrine'' (Ichinomiya Sengenjinja Some call it a kana (Japanese historical place name system, etc.) which is pronounced as 'Ichinomiya Asamajinja'.) is {{ill|lt=Yamanashi Prefecture|Q132720|qid=Q132720|1=Yamanashi Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Nishiyatsushiro District|Q1131467|qid=Q1131467|1=Nishiyatsushiro District, Yamanashi}}{{ill|lt=Ichikawa Misato Town|Q1204453|qid=Q1204453|1=Ichikawamisato}}{{ill|lt=Shrine|Q845945|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Takada. {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}} ({{ill|lt=Meishin Taisha|Q9610964|qid=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}}) {{ill|lt=Ronsha|Q135022904|qid=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}}, {{ill|lt=Modern shrine rank system|Q712534|qid=Q712534|lt=Old shrine rank|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} is {{ill|lt=Murasha|Q134917284|qid=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. The enshrinement site is located south of Takada village, at the northwestern tip of the Nishiyashiro Plateau, and in ancient times the shrine was simply called ``Ichinomiya'', ``Ichinomiya Myojin'', and ``Ichikawa Ichinomiya''. === Enshrined God === ; Main enshrined deity * '''{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kohana Sakuyahime no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}''' (Konohana no Sakuyahime no Mikoto) ; Aidenjin * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Qiongine Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} (Ninigi no Mikoto) * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hikohohodemi Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} === History === [[File:Ichinomiya-sengen Shrine C.JPG|right|thumb|220px|Ichinomiya Sengen Shrine Torii]]According to the shrine's legend, it was founded in the morning of {{ill|lt=Emperor Keiko|Q329723|qid=Q329723|1=Emperor Keikō}}Norio Saito's "Ichinomiya Sengen Shrine" (included in "Japanese Gods"). . (Japan)|Q1075635|Jōgan}} In response to the {{ill|lt=Jogan Great Eruption|Q11634546|qid=Q11634546|1=Jōgan eruption of Mount Fuji}} of {{ill|lt=Mt. Fuji|Q39231|qid=Q39231|1=Mount Fuji}} that occurred in (Japan)||In order to appease the divine will of God}}, it was founded in {{ill|lt=Yatsushiro District (Kaikoku)|Q11390569|qid=Q11390569|lt=Yatsushiro District, Kai Province|1=Yatsushiro district}} by imperial order in the following year {{ill|lt=865|Q30350|qid=Q30350|1=865}} (7th year of Jogan), and was designated as a government shrine and {{ill|lt=(Shinto priest)|Q24869239|qid=Q24869239|lt=Kogi|1=Hōri}} and {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Negi|1=UNKNOWN}} are said to have been placed in the shrine of the Sengen Myojin. , it is {{ill|lt=Meishin Taisha|Q9610964|qid=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}} ``Sengen Shrine'' in Yatsushiro District, Kai Province, which is listed in ``{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932|qid=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}'', and the old name of ``Ichinomiya'' is said to have come from the fact that it was called {{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q1656379|qid=Q1656379|1=Ichinomiya}} in Kai Province. However, the comparison of "Sengen Shrine" of Shikinai Meishin Taisha is {{jalink|Sengen Shrine of {{ill|lt=Fuefuki City|Q849434|qid=Q849434|1=Fuefuki}} (Fuefuki City)|Q11557476|Sengen Shrine}} or the Sengen Shrine ({{ill|lt=Kawaguchi Sengen Shrine|Q11553669|qid=Q11553669|1=Kawaguchi Sengen Shrine}}) in {{ill|lt=Fujikawaguchiko Town|Q1004231|qid=Q1004231|1=Fujikawaguchiko}} is considered to be a likely candidate.See "{{ill|lt=Kawaguchi Sengen Shrine|Q11553669|qid=Q11553669|1=Kawaguchi Sengen Shrine}}" for details. , Kai Province Ichinomiya is commonly referred to as Fuefuki City Sengen Shrine, so the name ``Ichinomiya'' is also thought to be the name of {{ill|lt=Ichikawa-go|UNKNOWN|lt=Old Ichikawa-go}}, where it is enshrined. name="Hakusuisha"/>{{ill|lt=Junichi Kamata|Q11651081|qid=Q11651081|1=Jun'ichi Kamata}}"Sengen Shrine" ("Shikiuchisha Investigation Report" Volume 10). . In addition, {{ill|lt=Yuge Shrine (Ichikawa Misato Town)|UNKNOWN|lt=Yuge Shrine}} called "Ninomiya" is located 750 {{ill|lt=meter|Q11573|qid=Q11573|1=Metre}} away, but {{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q1656379|qid=Q1656379|lt=Ninomiya|1=Ichinomiya}} in Kai Province is {{jalink|Miwa Shrine} in Fuefuki City. (Fuefuki City)|Q11608850|Miwa Shrine}}, so it is thought that this was also the name of ``Ninomiya'' for this shrine, which was also the first shrine of Ichikawa-go Junichi Kamata, ``Yuge Shrine'' (``Shikiuchi Shrine Investigation Report'', Vol. 10). (Also see {{ill|lt=Asama Shrine #Kai Province|Q3471785|qid=Q3471785|1=Sengen shrine}}).Jogan was founded on Mt. Mishotaizan in the southern part of the current enshrinement site of Konohana Sakuya Hime no Mikoto, the god of Mt. Fuji. ink|Mt. Gosatai|Q11489140}}, it is a general term for several peaks south of Takada and west of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Obina Pass|1=UNKNOWN}} (Takada Slope), and is also called ``Mt. Seitai'' (``Nishi Yatsushiro Gunshi'', Nishi Yatsushiro County Office, Yamanashi Prefecture, 1912 (Meiji 45)). It is said that the reason for this was that Kadokawa was relocated to Japan in order to escape from the eruption. ``Place name of Yamanashi prefecture''. , then {{ill|lt=924|Q23651|qid=Q23651|1=924}} ({{ill|lt=extended (era name)|Q1327274|qid=Q1327274|lt=extended|1=Enchō}}2 years), the shrine was relocated to the current site, which was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Satonomiya|1=UNKNOWN}} against Mt. Gosatai. , the Yamagisha Shrine was dedicated as {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yamiya|1=UNKNOWN}} on the former site of the mountain. According to "{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kaikoku Shrine and Temple Records|1=UNKNOWN}}" (hereinafter referred to as "Shaki"), {{ill|lt=1868|Q7717|qid=Q7717|1=1868}} ({{ill|lt=Keio|Q776775|qid=Q776775|1=Keiō}}A compilation of written records of the history and traditions of shrines and temples in Yamanashi Prefecture that were submitted to the Kofuji Shrine General Manager between July 1994 and October 1987 (renamed in September 1994). , Asano's reverence extended to October 2016 (924), {{ill|lt=Eiman|Q1193868|qid=Q1193868|1=Eiman}} November 3rd year (1167-1168) However, Eiman's name was changed to {{ill|lt=Jin'an (Japan)|Q3577285|qid=Q3577285|lt=Jin'an|1=Nin'an}} on June 5, 2013. , {{ill|lt=1186|Q19730|qid=Q19730|1=1186}} ({{ill|lt=Bunji|Q1194415|qid=Q1194415|1=Bunji (era)}}2 April), {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=1382|1=UNKNOWN}} ({{ill|lt=Eitoku|Q1145805|qid=Q1145805|1=Eitoku}}October 2012) is known for the construction of the shrine, and also the construction of {{ill|lt=Shinryō|UNKNOWN|lt=Shinryōchi}}30{{ill|lt=town (unit)|Q1185752|qid=Q1185752|lt=town|1=chō}} (approximately 3,600{{ill|lt=tsubo|Q706688|qid=Q706688|1=Pyeong}}), and 12 {{ill|lt=shake|Q11588367|qid=Q11588367|1=Shake (social class)}} people belonged to .After that, the {{ill|lt=Takeda clan#KaiTakeda clan|Q1210114|qid=Q1210114|lt=Takeda|1=Takeda clan}} targeted the {{ill|lt=priests|Q11589454|qid=Q11589454|1=Shinkan (official)}} of the shrines in Kai Province to Fuchu Hachiman Shrine (currently {{ill|lt=Kofu City|Q201932|qid=Q201932|1=Kōfu}} {{jalink|Hachiman Shrine}). (Miyamae-cho, Kofu City||Hachiman Shrine}}) was ordered to work for two days and two nights, but the ``Ichinomiya'', which appears as ``Ichinomiya no Negi'' in number 45 of the ``Fuchu Hachimansha Kinbancho (Takeda Clan Bankbook)'', is thought to be this shrine. This booklet was attached to the prohibition by {{ill|lt=Takeda Shingen|Q276404|qid=Q276404|1=Takeda Shingen}} that was issued to Fuchu Hachiman Shrine on March 11th of the same year, and the number of priests targeted was 164 shrines. Furthermore, ``Ichinomiya no Negi,'' who appears at number 44 in a similar directory (covering 160 shrines) in {{ill|lt=1608|Q6758|qid=Q6758|1=1608}} ({{ill|lt=Keichō|Q1328185|qid=Q1328185|1=Keichō}}13), is also said to be a priest of this shrine (``Place Names of Yamanashi Prefecture''). . According to the "Shaki", in {{ill|lt=1582|Q6719|qid=Q6719|1=1582}} ({{ill|lt=Tensho|Q1145058|qid=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} 10th year), {{ill|lt=Tokugawa Ieyasu|Q171977|qid=Q171977|1=Tokugawa Ieyasu}} entered Kai and set up camp at this shrine, and during the war the shrine territory was confiscated and the shrine was left in ruins for a time, but , it seems to have been rebuilt afterwards, in {{ill|lt=1603|Q6735|qid=Q6735|1=1603}} ({{ill|lt=Keicho|Q1328185|qid=Q1328185|1=Keichō}}8) is the {{ill|lt=Black Seal|Q11678034|qid=Q11678034|1=black seal}} of {{ill|lt=Four Tokugawa Magistrates|Q11490263|qid=Q11490263|1=Four Tokugawa Magistrates}}, with 1 {{jalink|stone} in Ichikawa Takada Village (present-day Takada). (Unit)|Q855048|stone}}6{{ill|lt=to|Q1078168|qid=Q1078168|1=To (unit)}}5{{ill|lt=sho|Q995076|qid=Q995076|1=Sheng (volume)}}, 260 tsubo is {{jali nk|Relief|Q11450498}}, and it is said that Ieyasu also donated {{ill|lt=karigi|Q5361852|qid=Q5361852|1=kariginu}} and {{ill|lt=tachi|Q120676|qid=Q120676|1=Tachi}}, {{ill|lt=1649|Q6945|qid=Q6945|1=1649}} ({{ill|lt=Keian|Q1146135|qid=Q1146135|1=Keian}}2 years) was renewed from {{ill|lt=Tokugawa Shogun|Q14916275|qid=Q14916275|lt=Shogun|1=Tokugawa shogun}}{{ill|lt=Tokugawa Iemitsu|Q314481|qid=Q314481|1=Tokugawa Iemitsu}} to 1 koku 6 todo. jalink|Shuin territory}} was relieved, and the bamboo forest and {{ill|lt=Shinto priest|Q15888735|qid=Q15888735|1=Kannushi (disambiguation)}} mansion in the shrine territory were made {{ill|lt=cleared land|Q28686012|qid=Q28686012|1=cleared land}} exempt from various duties, and they were maintained throughout the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|qid=Q184963|1=Edo period}}. name="Kadokawa place name"/>. Also, in {{ill|lt=1643|Q6917|qid=Q6917|1=1643}} ({{ill|lt=Kanei|Q1192913|qid=Q1192913|1=Kan'ei}} 20th year), the 8th {{jalink|Prince} of {{ill|lt=Emperor Goyoze|Q349440|qid=Q349440|1=Emperor Go-Yōzei}}|Q1288 It is said that {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Imperial Prince Yoshizumi|1=UNKNOWN}}, who was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=banished|1=UNKNOWN}} to Kai Province in 6381}}, was also worshiped here and dedicated a plaque, etc. "{{ill|lt=Kokushi|Q11577871|qid=Q11577871|1=Kaikokushi}}". .At the beginning of the {{ill|lt=Meiji period|Q3125096|qid=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} (mid-{{ill|lt=19th century|Q6955|qid=Q6955|1=19th century}}), they lined up at village shrines. === Precincts === * {{ill|lt=main hall|Q2044994|qid=Q2044994|1=Honden}} :: Reconstruction of {{ill|lt=1703|Q6822|qid=Q6822|1=1703}} ({{ill|lt=Genroku|Q1325001|qid=Q1325001|1=Genroku}}16). {{ill|lt=Nagaruzou|Q210684|qid=Q210684|lt=Sangensha Nagaruzo|1=Nagare-zukuri}} {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=copper plate roofing|1=UNKNOWN}} with 5 girders and 3 beams {{ill|lt=irimoya-zukuri|Q855025|qid=Q855025|1=East Asian hip-and-gable roof}} It is connected to the {{ill|lt=haiden|Q3125745|qid=Q3125745|1=Haiden (Shinto)}} with {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=tiled roof|1=UNKNOWN}} and a corridor with one beam and two beams. File:Ichinomiya-sengen Shrine B.JPG|Roumon (left) and worship hall === Setsusha === When the {{ill|lt=Great Ansei Earthquake#A series of great earthquakes at the end of the Edo period|Q849463|qid=Q849463|lt=Great Earthquakes|1=Ansei great earthquakes}} struck the area in {{ill|lt=1854|Q7670|qid=Q7670|1=1854}} ({{ill|lt=Ansei|Q1327259|qid=Q1327259|1=Ansei}} first year), he made efforts to help the victims. There is also a branch shrine that originated from the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ikushō|1=UNKNOWN}} where {{ill|lt=daikan|Q10263740|qid=Q10263740|1=Daikan}} {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kendo Arai|1=UNKNOWN}} and {{ill|lt=headman|Q110095935|qid=Q110095935|1=myōshu}} Yasukiyo Yoda were enshrined during their lifetimes, as well as five other shrines. According to the "Shaki," the old shrines were Misaki Daimyojin in Shimo-Otorii Village (currently {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Misaki Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} in Shimo-Otorii, Ichikawa Misato Town), Sutaka Daimyojin in Kurosawa Village (currently {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sutaka Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} in Kurosawa, Ichikawa Town), and Sanno Gongen (currently {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hiei Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} in Kurosawa, Ichikawa Village). (Ichikawa Misato Town)||Hiei Shrine}}) and other nearby shrines were named {{ill|lt=Setsusha|Q135009904|qid=Q135009904|1=Sessha}}. === Festival === * {{ill|lt=Regular Festival|Q11385469|qid=Q11385469|1=Reisai}} ({{ill|lt=November 3rd|Q2995|qid=Q2995|1=November 3}}) : According to the "Shaki", it used to be that November's Dragon's Day was a national holiday, and on that day, a mikoshi (portable shrine) was carried from Mt. Gosatai. Also, during the Takeda clan era, a rice festival was held on April tatsuhi, and after that, Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine in Oi (now {{ill|lt=Minami Alps City|Q824000|qid=Q824000|1=Minami-Alps, Yamanashi}} Tokaichi Market, about 6 {{ill|lt=kilometers|Q828224|qid=Q828224|1=Kilometre}} north of {{ill|lt=Koma District|Q11479803|qid=Q11479803|1=Koma District}} It is said that a mikoshi was passed to (Minami Alps City) Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine (Minami Alps City), where feudal lords joined in the procession, and cavalry warriors also assembled their horses, and the area came to be known as ``Twenty Horse Fields.'' It is also said that a market was built on the land where the mikoshi was parked, and that is where the name of the current Minami Alps City Old Market came from. In addition, there was a ritual for the first harvest on September 19th that began in {{ill|lt=1635|Q6879|qid=Q6879|1=1635}} (Kanei 12th year), but most of these rituals have ceased to exist. === Cultural assets === (specified type and date in parentheses) === Important cultural property (designated by the country) === [[File:Opposed divine beast mirror excavated from Toriihara Tumulus.JPG|thumb|180px|right|{{center|Opposed divine beast mirror
(National {{ill|lt=Important Cultural Property|Q1188622|qid=Q1188622|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}})}}{{small|{{ill|lt=Tokyo National Museum|Q653433|qid=Q653433|1=Tokyo National Museum}} exhibition. }}]] * Divine beast mirror 1 (archaeological material, {{ill|lt=1979|Q2484|qid=Q2484|1=1979}} ({{ill|lt=Showa|Q327567|qid=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}}54) June 6) :{{ill|lt=1894|Q7843|qid=Q7843|1=1894}} (Meiji 27), this bronze mirror (four gods and four beasts mirror) was excavated from {{ill|lt=Toriihara Foxzuka Tumulus|Q11674888|qid=Q11674888|1=Toriibara Kitsunezuka Kofun}} in Otsuka, Ichikawa Misato Town, and dedicated to this shrine. It has been reported by Takashi Okazaki that statues of the four gods and four beasts are arranged alternately in the inner ward of the mirror back, and on the outer ward, the inscription ``'''May 5th day of the 1st year of Akagarasu, Zosukaku Meisho, Hyakko Kiyodo, Prince of Clothes, Descendants of Nobu, 10,000 years of life''' has been cast. This divine beast mirror was reported to the academic society by {{ill|lt=Moruichi Goto|Q11488456|qid=Q11488456|1=Shūichi Gotō}} in {{ill|lt=1924|Q2173|qid=Q2173|1=1924}} ({{ill|lt=Taisho|Q329174|qid=Q329174|1=Taishō era}}13).:"{{ill|lt=Red Crow|Q8011978|qid=Q8011978|1=Chiwu}}" written in the inscription is {{jalink|Wu of {{ill|lt=Three Kingdoms Period (China)|Q185043|qid=Q185043|lt=Three Kingdoms Period|1=Three Kingdoms}} (Mikuni)|Q274488|Wu}}'s {{ill|lt=era name|Q256408|qid=Q256408|1=Era name}}, which corresponds to {{ill|lt=238 years|Q30058|qid=Q30058|lt=238 AD|1=238}}, It is valuable as the oldest of Japan's era-inscribed mirrors, and as a relic that suggests the possibility of negotiations between Japan ({{ill|lt=Wa|Q1378053|qid=Q1378053|1=Wa (name of Japan)}}) and Wu at the time. In 1933 (Showa 8), it was recognized as {{ill|lt=Important Art Object|Q11644862|qid=Q11644862|1=Important Art Object}}, and in 1979, it was again designated as an Important Cultural Property. (1976) Ministry of Education Notification No. 112, June 6, [https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/9991 Agency for Cultural Affairs, “Nationally Designated Cultural Properties Database”] (accessed March 23, 2010). . === Yamanashi Prefecture designated cultural property === *1 bronze mirror (tangible cultural property (craft), {{ill|lt=1968|Q2644|qid=Q2644|1=1968}} (February 8, 1968)) :A {{ill|lt=manufactured mirror|Q130529515|qid=Q130529515|lt=manufactured|1=Bōseikyō}} {{ill|lt=bronze mirror|Q1041984|qid=Q1041984|lt=bronze mirror|1=Bronze mirror}} that was excavated along with the above divine beast mirror. The flat border of the plain text has a double comb-tooth pattern that divides it into inner and outer wards. . === Others === *{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Zuijin|1=UNKNOWN}} 2 statues :{{ill|lt=Attack|Attack|qid=Attack|ltq=Q19598808}}{{ill|lt=Sokutai|Q1156828|qid=Q1156828|lt=Sokutai figure|1=Sokutai}}. On the back of the {{ill|lt=Ungata|Q137190286|qid=Q137190286|1=Ungyō}} statue, there is an inscription written in ink by {{ill|lt=1557|Q6595|qid=Q6595|1=1557}} ({{ill|lt=Koji (Japan)|Q1143910|qid=Q1143910|lt=Koji|1=Kōji (Muromachi period)}}), making it a valuable piece of medieval work inscribed in Yamanashi Prefecture, which is relatively rare. *Ichinomiya Sengen Miyacho :Edo period {{ill|lt=Chronicle|Q185363|qid=Q185363|1=Chronicle}}. Paper and ink calligraphy. Dimensions are 24.8 cm long and 16.9 cm wide. Total of 7 books. The seventh book states that the shogunate was responsible for the {{ill|lt=flood|Q8068|qid=Q8068|1=Flood}} that occurred in Kai in {{ill|lt=Enkyo|Q1183728|qid=Q1183728|1=Enkyō (Edo period)}}4 ({{ill|lt=1747|Q7596|qid=Q7596|1=1747}}). jalink|Tottori Domain|Q905521}} and {{ill|lt=Oka Domain|Q8192275|qid=Q8192275|1=Oka Domain}} are known to be ordered to carry out restoration work. === Local information === ; Location * {{ill|lt=Yamanashi Prefecture|Q132720|qid=Q132720|1=Yamanashi Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Nishiyashiro District|Q1131467|qid=Q1131467|1=Nishiyatsushiro District, Yamanashi}}{{ill|lt=Ichikawa Misato Town|Q1204453|qid=Q1204453|1=Ichikawamisato}}3696 Miyamoto, Takada ; Transportation access * Nearest station: {{ill|lt=Central Railway|Q513679|qid=Q513679|1=Central Japan Railway Company}} (JR Tokai) {{ill|lt=Minobu Line|Q162747|qid=Q162747|1=Minobu Line}} {{ill|lt=Ichikawa Daimon Station|Q5986151|qid=Q5986151|1=Ichikawa-Daimon Station}} (about 12 minutes walk) === Footnotes === {{reflist|2}} === References === *Shikinaisha Research Report, edited by Shikinaisha Research Association, Volume 10, Tokaido 5 (Izu Province, Kai Province), Kogakkan University Publishing Department, 1981 (Showa 56) *Kenichi Tanigawa (ed.) Japanese Gods-Shrines and Sacred Sites Volume 10 Tokai [Reprinted], Hakusuisha, 2000 ISBN 978-4-560-02510-9 (first published in 1987) *"Kadokawa Japanese Place Name Dictionary 19 Yamanashi Prefecture", Kadokawa Shoten, 1980 ISBN 978-4040011905 *"Place Names of Yamanashi Prefecture" (Japan Historical Place Names Series Volume 19), Heibonsha, 1995 ISBN 4-582-49019-0 === External link === {{commonscat|Ichinomiyasengen-jinja}} * {{Shinto/Shrine Historical Materials Collection|146001|Sengen Shrine (Yatsushiro District, Kai Province)}} * {{Cultural Heritage Online|168845|Divine Beast Mirror / Inscribed on the 5th day of May 2016}} {{Shinto horizontal}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Ichinomiya Sengen Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Ichinomiya Sengen Shrine]][[Category:Asama shrines]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Ichinomiya Sengen Shrine]][[Category:Shikinai Successor]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kai Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:浅間神社]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kai Province]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Ichinomiya (regional)]] [[Category:Son-sha in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Yamanashi prefecture]] [[Category:市川三郷町の建築物]] [[Category:市川三郷町の歴史]]
